title: Coriandrum cicuta
intro:
There is no information available about the plant Coriandrum cicuta. However, I can provide you with information about the plant Coriandrum sativum, also known as coriander or cilantro.

Coriandrum sativum is a plant in the parsley family, native to the Mediterranean region and Middle East, with seeds and leaves used in cooking, and seeds also used in herbal medicine.

The health benefits of coriander include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce nausea and vomiting, improve digestion, and lower blood sugar levels. The bioactive constituents of coriander include limonene, linalool, and borneol, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

Coriander is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as tea, capsules, and essential oil, which can be used to support digestive health and reduce anxiety and stress.
properties: Coriandrum cicuta is not a widely recognized species of plant, and information about its therapeutic properties could not be found. Further research is needed to confirm the specific therapeutic properties of Coriandrum cicuta.
